content:
Department Focus: Resources & Finance at Hyndburn

Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council's Resources & Finance department accounted for £119.0M of spending over the analysed period.
  • This expenditure represents 56.3% of the council's total analysed spend of £211.3M.
  • Environmental Services was the second highest spending department, with an outlay of £34.8M, making up 16.5% of the total.
  • Regeneration & Housing followed as the third largest spending department, with costs of £31.5M, or 14.9% of the total.

Hyndburn's Overall Financial Landscape

Hyndburn Borough Council's spending data for the period 2016/17 to 2025/26 reveals a total expenditure of £211.3M. A significant portion of this budget, specifically £119.0M, was allocated to the Resources & Finance department. This substantial figure highlights the central role this department plays within the council's operations.

The Resources & Finance department is responsible for critical functions that underpin the entire council. These typically include financial management, budgeting, accounting, human resources, information technology, procurement, and property management. Its expenditure of £119.0M, representing 56.3% of the council's total analysed spend, indicates its broad remit and essential support services for all other council departments.

Departmental Spending Across the Council

An analysis of Hyndburn Borough Council's spending shows how its total budget of £211.3M is distributed across various departments. The Resources & Finance department stands out as the largest spender, but other departments also manage significant budgets to deliver public services. Understanding this distribution provides insight into the council's priorities and operational structure.

Beyond Resources & Finance, two other departments account for substantial portions of the council's expenditure. Environmental Services spent £34.8M, representing 16.5% of the total budget. Regeneration & Housing followed with an expenditure of £31.5M, which is 14.9% of the total. These figures demonstrate the council's commitment to maintaining local environments and supporting community development initiatives.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Resources & Finance £119.0M 56.3%
2 Environmental Services £34.8M 16.5%
3 Regeneration & Housing £31.5M 14.9%
4 Other £17.3M 8.2%
5 Legal & Democratic £3.6M 1.7%
6 Planning & Transportation £2.9M 1.4%
7 Environmental Health £2.2M 1.0%

The Role of Resources & Finance in Council Operations

The dominance of Resources & Finance spending, at £119.0M or 56.3% of the total, underscores its foundational importance. This department ensures the financial stability and compliance of the council, manages its workforce, and provides the technological infrastructure necessary for modern public service delivery. Without a robust Resources & Finance function, other departments would struggle to operate effectively and deliver their services to the community.

While Resources & Finance manages the largest share, other departments contribute significantly to the council's diverse service portfolio. Legal & Democratic services spent £3.6M, or 1.7%, ensuring governance and legal compliance. Planning & Transportation allocated £2.9M, or 1.4%, for local development and infrastructure. Environmental Health, with £2.2M, or 1.0%, focuses on public health and safety. These varied expenditures collectively enable Hyndburn Borough Council to serve its residents across many critical areas, all supported by the central financial and administrative functions.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about the specific breakdown of spending within the Resources & Finance department.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions about budget allocations and departmental performance.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand which suppliers are awarded contracts by various departments, including Resources & Finance.
  • Write to your local councillor about the significant proportion of spending allocated to central services and ask how this expenditure directly benefits local residents.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Hyndburn Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
